title: Subscription Trial Cycling for Zero-Dollar Card Validation
narrative: Fraudsters exploit free trial periods on streaming services, SaaS platforms, and digital subscriptions to validate stolen card credentials without triggering $0.01-$1.00 authorization charges that banks often flag. Attackers register 15-40 accounts within 2-6 hours using sequential email variations (servicename+001@domain.com through servicename+040@domain.com) from disposable providers like guerrillamail or tempmail. Each card undergoes a $0.00 authorization hold during trial signup, confirming validity for subsequent fraud on higher-value merchants. Successful validations show card1_txn_count_1day jumping from 0 to 20+ within the test window, all with identical P_emaildomain but rotating addr1 values. The subscription MCC (5968 or 7372) combined with ProductCD='S' and dist1 values exceeding 5000km between sequential transactions reveals the coordinated testing operation before cards are monetized elsewhere.
indicators: 15-40 transactions with TransactionAmt=$0.00 or $0.01 from single card within 2-6 hours
P_emaildomain matches disposable providers (guerrillamail.com, tempmail.net, 10minutemail) with sequential username patterns
card1_txn_count_1day increases from 0 to 20+ during testing window
All transactions share MCC 5968 or 7372 (subscription services) with ProductCD='S'
dist1 values exceed 5000km between consecutive transactions despite same card1
Rotating addr1 values (10+ unique) while device fingerprints remain constant
Burst occurs during txn_hour 2-7 UTC (is_night=1 for US merchants) suggesting automated scripting
summary: A single card creates 15-40 zero-dollar subscription trial signups within a few hours, using sequential disposable emails and rotating addresses while device fingerprints stay identical. Transaction distances jump thousands of kilometers between attempts despite the same card.
reasoning: Criminals validate stolen card credentials through free trials that avoid typical authorization flags, testing which cards are active before using them for high-value fraud elsewhere. The automated bursts with disposable emails reveal coordinated testing operations rather than legitimate customer behavior.
indicator_explanations: Card is being slammed with dozens of zero-dollar subscription trials
Email addresses rotate through temporary throwaway services in sequence
Card activity explodes from zero to dozens of transactions today
All hits target subscription services using the same product type
Purchases appear in physically impossible locations seconds apart
Billing addresses cycle through many variants while device stays fixed
Activity concentrates in overnight hours suggesting bot-driven automation
